sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content: Surgery is the primary treatment for most localized and resectable MCC. It is essential for both treatment and accurate pathologic staging (primary tumor and SLNB).

cards.name: Wide Local Excision (WLE)
cards.detail:
Approach: Standard excision with margins.
Indication: Standard approach for primary tumor excision.
Key complications: Bleeding, infection, wound dehiscence, cosmetic/functional morbidity, lymphedema if near nodal basin.
Operative mortality: Not specified in text.

cards.name: Mohs Micrographic Surgery or other forms of Peripheral and Deep En Face Margin Assessment (PDEMA)
cards.detail:
Approach: Staged excision with intraoperative margin assessment, tissue conservation.
Indication: May be appropriate in certain circumstances, especially for cosmetically sensitive areas, to ensure clear margins while sparing tissue. Must not interfere with SLNB [MCC-2, footnote j; MCC-C].
Key complications: Similar to WLE; requires specialized pathologist training.
Operative mortality: Not specified in text.

cards.name: Sentinel Lymph Node Biopsy (SLNB)
cards.detail:
Approach: Identification and excision of sentinel lymph node(s) with immunopanel staining.
Indication: Important staging tool for clinically node-negative disease. Recommended prior to or at the time of primary excision [MCC-C, footnote a].
Key complications: Lymphedema, seroma, nerve injury, false-negative results (reported rates 17-21%).
Operative mortality: Not specified in text.

cards.name: Therapeutic Lymph Node Dissection (TLND)
cards.detail:
Approach: Surgical removal of regional lymph nodes.
Indication: For biopsy-proven regional nodal metastases. Part of preferred management of the draining nodal basin (node dissection + RT) [MCC-4].
Key complications: Lymphedema (significant risk, especially groin), seroma, nerve injury, infection.
Operative mortality: Not specified in text.
